text:
In the quiet town of Everwood, nestled between rolling hills and whispering woods, lived a young girl named Willow Honeybanks. She possessed an insatiable curiosity about everything—an oak tree, a rainbow, an antique piano. But Willow had one beloved obsession that seemed endless: classic comedies.

Every weekend without fail, Willow would pour herself into watching classic movie marathons. Her favorite? Charlie Chaplin. She knew every line by heart. When summer rolls around, she’ll often surprise her friends with her chatty commentary during old film marathons.

Once a year, the town hosted its favorite film festival. It was a highlight for everyone. Willow watched Robin Hood again with tears of joy. She giggled at jokes she couldn't understand. That night, as she crawled into bed dreaming of gallantly gallant heroes, Willow felt grateful she had such joy.

Years passed. Willow grew up. She worked as a librarian. She loved the history behind the films. She knew
